title: secure_zero: word-aligned path rechecks alignment
description:
In lichen_util.h:51, the word-aligned write loop redundantly checks alignment:

```c
while (len >= sizeof(unsigned long) &&
       ((uintptr_t)p & (sizeof(unsigned long) - 1)) == 0) {
```

After the initial alignment loop (lines 46-49), p is guaranteed word-aligned. The second alignment check inside the bulk loop is dead code - it will always be true if execution reaches this point.

What goes wrong: Nothing functionally breaks, but the check runs every iteration for buffers >= 32 bytes. On a hot path (mutex-protected key wiping), this adds measurable overhead.

Suggested fix: Remove the redundant alignment check from the bulk loop condition.
